findings.evidence: `rg -n "finite_compl_fixedBy_(closure|swap)|IsSwap|closure|swap" code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/FiniteSupportPerm.lean code/TauCeti/Probability/Exchangeability/PathSpace/ExchangeableSigma.lean` has no hits; the actual uses are at `FiniteSupportPerm.lean:23-57` for `MulAction.fixedBy`, `.bddAbove`, `Set.finite_Iio`, and `Equiv.Perm.mul_apply`.
findings.fix: Replace it with the narrower Mathlib imports that provide `MulAction.fixedBy`/`mem_fixedBy` and permutation application, e.g. `Mathlib.GroupTheory.GroupAction.FixedPoints` plus the needed permutation support module, keeping `Data.Set.Finite.Lattice` and the interval import as needed.
findings.issue: `public import Mathlib.GroupTheory.Perm.ClosureSwap` pulls in the transposition-closure API, but this file only uses fixed-point membership for permutations, finite set boundedness, and finite intervals.

summary: The new declarations are now in plausible homes, including the generic finite-support bridges split out of the path-space σ-algebra file. One import in that bridge file is still evidently overbroad for the material it contains.
